title: Malache spinifex

Malache spinifex is often placed in the family Droseraceae, which is a family of sundews and other similar carnivorous plants. side_effects: Malache spinifex, also known as prickly poppy, is a plant native to Australia and parts of Asia. While it has been used in traditional medicine, its use can lead to adverse effects, including gastrointestinal issues, allergic reactions, and skin irritation. Ingesting the plant's seeds can cause vomiting and diarrhea in humans and animals. Topical application of the plant's sap can result in skin burns and blisters.
